evidence.evidence_text: Durability of concrete pipes subjected to combined steam and carbonation curing Vahid Rostami, Yixin Shao ⇑, Andrew J. Boyd Department of Civil Engineering and Applied Mechanics, McGill University, 817 Sherbrooke Street West, Montreal, Quebec, Canada H3A 2K6 article info Article history: Received 22 October 2010 Received in revised form 10 February 2011 Accepted 1 March 2011 Available online 27 March 2011 Keywords: Carbonation Steam curing Concrete pipe Durability sulfate resistance abstract A b
Early and late strength of carbonated and steam hydrated concretes are shown in Figs. 4 and 5; packet text does not provide numeric values.

review_queue.question: Digitize or inspect the source figure/table to recover mixture-level compressive-strength values.
review_queue.suggested_action: Open the PDF page images and extract numeric strength values only if mixture labels, age, unit, and values can be confirmed.

main_risks: Compressive-strength values are not directly readable from packet text.
